Name: UParticleSystem
Description:
A ParticleSystem is a complete particle effect that contains any number of ParticleEmitters. By allowing multiple emitters
  in a system, the designer can create elaborate particle effects that are held in a single system. Once created using
  Cascade, a ParticleSystem can then be inserted into a level or created in script.

Variables.Name: UpdateTime_FPS
Variables.Description: UpdateTime_FPS	- the frame per second to update at in FixedTime mode

Variables.Name: UpdateTime_Delta
Variables.Description: UpdateTime_Delta	- internal

Variables.Name: WarmupTime
Variables.Description:
WarmupTime - the time to warm-up the particle system when first rendered	
	  Warning: WarmupTime is implemented by simulating the particle system for the time requested upon activation.  
	  This is extremely prone to cause hitches, especially with large particle counts - use with caution.

Variables.Name: WarmupTickRate
Variables.Description:
WarmupTickRate - the time step for each tick during warm up.
		Set to 0 to use the default tick time.

Variables.Name: Emitters
Variables.Description: Emitters	- internal - the array of emitters in the system

Variables.Name: PreviewComponent
Variables.Description: The component used to preview the particle system in Cascade

Variables.Name: CurveEdSetup
Variables.Description: Used for curve editor to remember curve-editing setup.

Variables.Name: bOrientZAxisTowardCamera
Variables.Description: If true, the system's Z axis will be oriented toward the camera

Variables.Name: LODDistanceCheckTime
Variables.Description: How often (in seconds) the system should perform the LOD distance check.

Variables.Name: bUseAbsoluteDistance
Variables.Description: default false ,use for cull distance not affected by r.ViewDistanceScale

Variables.Name: CustomCullDistance
Variables.Description: default 0 ,use for mobile particle distance cull

Variables.Name: CustomPCCullDistance
Variables.Description: default -1 then use same distance as mobile do

Variables.Name: LODMethod
Variables.Description:
The method of LOD level determination to utilize for this particle system
	 	  PARTICLESYSTEMLODMETHOD_Automatic - Automatically set the LOD level, checking every LODDistanceCheckTime seconds.
	     PARTICLESYSTEMLODMETHOD_DirectSet - LOD level is directly set by the game code.
	     PARTICLESYSTEMLODMETHOD_ActivateAutomatic - LOD level is determined at Activation time, then left alone unless directly set by game code.

Variables.Name: LODDistances
Variables.Description:
The array of distances for each LOD level in the system.
	 	Used when LODMethod is set to PARTICLESYSTEMLODMETHOD_Automatic.
	 
	 	Example: System with 3 LOD levels
	 		LODDistances(0) = 0.0
	 		LODDistances(1) = 2500.0
	 		LODDistances(2) = 5000.0
	 
	 		In this case, when the system is [   0.0 ..   2499.9] from the camera, LOD level 0 will be used.
	 										 [2500.0 ..   4999.9] from the camera, LOD level 1 will be used.
	 										 [5000.0 .. INFINITY] from the camera, LOD level 2 will be used.

Variables.Name: bRegenerateLODDuplicate
Variables.Description:
Internal value that tracks the regenerate LOD levels preference.
	 	If true, when autoregenerating LOD levels in code, the low level will
	 	be a duplicate of the high.

Variables.Name: bUseFixedRelativeBoundingBox
Variables.Description: Whether to use the fixed relative bounding box or calculate it every frame.

Variables.Name: FixedRelativeBoundingBox
Variables.Description: Fixed relative bounding box for particle system.

Variables.Name: SecondsBeforeInactive
Variables.Description:
Number of seconds of emitter not being rendered that need to pass before it
	  no longer gets ticked becomes inactive.

Variables.Name: bShouldResetPeakCounts
Variables.Description: EDITOR ONLY: Indicates that Cascade would like to have the PeakActiveParticles count reset

Variables.Name: bHasPhysics
Variables.Description: Set during load time to indicate that physics is used...

Variables.Name: bUseRealtimeThumbnail
Variables.Description: Inidicates the old 'real-time' thumbnail rendering should be used

Variables.Name: ThumbnailImageOutOfDate
Variables.Description: Internal: Indicates the PSys thumbnail image is out of date

Variables.Name: Delay
Variables.Description: How long this Particle system should delay when ActivateSystem is called on it.

Variables.Name: DelayLow
Variables.Description: The low end of the emitter delay if using a range.

Variables.Name: bUseDelayRange
Variables.Description:
If true, select the emitter delay from the range 
	 		[DelayLow..Delay]

Variables.Name: InsignificantReaction
Variables.Description: The reaction this system takes when all emitters are insignificant.

Variables.Name: InsignificanceDelay
Variables.Description: Time delay between all emitters becoming insignificant and the systems insignificant reaction.

Variables.Name: MaxSignificanceLevel
Variables.Description: The maximum level of significance for emitters in this system. Any emitters with a higher significance will be capped at this significance level.

Variables.Name: MacroUVPosition
Variables.Description: Local space position that UVs generated with the ParticleMacroUV material node will be centered on.

Variables.Name: MacroUVRadius
Variables.Description: World space radius that UVs generated with the ParticleMacroUV material node will tile based on.

Variables.Name: OcclusionBoundsMethod
Variables.Description:
Which occlusion bounds method to use for this particle system.
	 	EPSOBM_None - Don't determine occlusion for this system.
	 	EPSOBM_ParticleBounds - Use the bounds of the component when determining occlusion.

Variables.Name: CustomOcclusionBounds
Variables.Description: The occlusion bounds to use if OcclusionBoundsMethod is set to EPSOBM_CustomBounds

Variables.Name: NamedMaterialSlots
Variables.Description:
Array of named material slots for use by emitters of this system. 
		Emitters can use these instead of their own materials by providing the name to the NamedMaterialOverrides property of their required module.
		These materials can be overridden using CreateNamedDynamicMaterialInstance() on a ParticleSystemComponent.
